name: Dough for Regular Crust Pizza
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Place the pizza baking stone or tiles in the oven to heat.
3. Dissolve the yeast in the warm (85°F) water.
4. Let stand without stirring until the mixture foams (8-10 minutes).
5. Combine the flour and salt in a large bowl.
6. Add the olive oil and yeast mixture. Stir well.
7. Add the grated Parmesan. Stir well.
8. Cover with a cloth and let rest about 10 minutes.
9. Turn out onto a well-floured board and knead until smooth (about 10 minutes).
10. Place in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise until doubled (about 2 hours).
11. Punch down. Turn out onto a floured board.
12. Divide the dough into equal portions.
13. Roll each portion of the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch.
14. Oil 14-inch pizza pans with olive oil.
15. Sprinkle some cornmeal on each pan.
16. Form the pies in the pans, pinching the edges up slightly to hold the filling.
17. Brush the dough generously with olive oil.
18. Spread sauce, seasonings, and toppings over the dough, leaving the outer 1/2 inch bare.
19. Let rest about 10 minutes.
20. Remove from pans using a pizza paddle and place on baking stones or tiles in the oven.
21. Bake until the edges are light brown and cheese is bubbly. Serve.
